Now let me check the runtime and services for the permission route:

No dedicated permissions route exists yet. Here's the **full API** based on the actual code:

---

## Full API Reference

All routes require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` **except** `GET /` and `GET /health`.

### Health & Discovery

| Method | Endpoint | Auth | Description |
|--------|----------|------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/` | ❌ | HTML landing page (gateway status dashboard) |
| `GET` | `/health` | ❌ | JSON health check → `{ ok: true, service: "claw-mobile-gateway" }` |

### Threads

| Method | Endpoint | Body | Response | Description |
|--------|----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/threads` | — | `{ threads: Thread[] }` | List all threads |
| `POST` | `/threads` | `{ title?, workDir? }` | `201 { thread }` | Create a thread (auto-generates timestamp title if omitted) |
| `PATCH` | `/threads/:threadId` | `{ workDir?, title? }` | `{ thread }` | Update thread metadata |
| `DELETE` | `/threads/:threadId` | — | `{ ok: true }` | Delete a thread |
| `POST` | `/threads/:threadId/duplicate` | — | `201 { thread }` | Duplicate a thread (copies messages, appends "(copy)" to title) |

### Messages

| Method | Endpoint | Body | Response | Description |
|--------|----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/threads/:threadId/messages` | — | `{ messages: Message[] }` | List messages in a thread |
| `POST` | `/threads/:threadId/messages` | `{ content, modelQueue?, model?, autoCompact?, streamingEnabled? }` | `202 { ok: true }` | Send a user message & trigger an AI run (async — returns immediately) |

**`modelQueue` schema:** array of `{ provider: "claude"|"openrouter"|"local", name?, apiKey? }` — falls back to single `model` if queue is empty.

### Streaming (SSE)

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/threads/:threadId/stream` | SSE subscription — realtime events for this thread |
| `POST` | `/threads/:threadId/stop` | Stop a running AI run |

**Note:** The SSE stream endpoint is registered in **both** `threads.ts` and `stream.ts` — the one in `stream.ts` sends the current thread status as the first event. In practice only one route matches per request (Express uses first match), so the `stream.ts` version is the active one.

**SSE event types:**

| Event | Payload | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `status` | `{ status: "idle"|"running"|"waiting"|"error" }` | Thread status change |
| `delta` | `{ messageId, chunk }` | Streaming text chunk for an assistant message |
| `message_error` | `{ messageId, text }` | Error in assistant message |
| `tool_start` | `{ id, tool, label, messageId }` | Agent started a tool execution |
| `tool_end` | `{ id, error? }` | Tool finished (or errored) |
| `permission_request` | `{ id, tool, description }` | Agent needs user approval (future) |
| `terminal` | `{ chunk }` | Terminal output chunk |
| `done` | — | Run completed |
| `error` | — | Run errored out |

### Terminal

| Method | Endpoint | Body | Response | Description |
|--------|----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/threads/:threadId/terminal` | — | `{ lines: string[] }` | Get terminal output history |
| `POST` | `/threads/:threadId/terminal` | `{ command }` | `{ ok: true }` | Send a terminal command |

### Filesystem

| Method | Endpoint | Query | Response | Description |
|--------|----------|-------|----------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/fs/browse` | `?path=<dir>` | `{ path, parent?, entries: [{ name, path, isDir }] }` | Browse remote filesystem (defaults to `$HOME`, dirs first then alphabetical, hidden files excluded) |

### Open App (Expo deep link)

| Method | Endpoint | Auth | Body | Description |
|--------|----------|------|------|-------------|
| `POST` | `/open-app` | Localhost only | `{ url }` (must be `exp://...`) | Register an Expo deep link for the gateway |
| `GET` | `/open-app` | ❌ | Redirect or HTML | Redirects to the registered `exp://` URL, or shows "not running" page |

---

That's everything — **14 endpoints** total across 7 route files. The `permission_request` SSE event type exists in the runtime but there's no REST endpoint yet for approving/denying permissions (that's a TODO).
